name: Morral de Pena-Roja and Voltes de la Llena Trail
name_original: Sierra de Tivissa - Circular por Ermita de Sant Blai
description: Hike the moderate 9.4-mile Morral de Pena-Roja and Voltes de la Llena Trail, featuring caves, viewpoints, and historic sites near Tivissa.
region_intro: Hiking around Tivissa offers diverse and rugged natural landscapes, characterized by karstic mountain ranges. The area features a wild Mediterranean environment with varied terrain, including springs, natural ponds, and plateaus. These geological formations provide panoramic views and a rich backdrop for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Tivissa The most popular hiking route is Morral de Pena-Roja and Voltes de la Llena Trail, a 9.4 miles (15.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 42 minutes to complete, featuring viewpoints and historic sites. Another top favourite among local hikers is Llena Caves – View of Tivissa loop from Tivissa, a moderate 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This route offers views of Tivissa and traverses the karstic landscape. Local hikers also love the La Miranda (918 m) – Tivissa Weather Radar loop from Llaberia, a 5.4 miles (8.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Hiking around Tivissa is defined by karstic mountain ranges, Mediterranean scenery, and diverse terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Tivissa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 280 reviews. More than 1100 hikers have used komoot to explore Tivissa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Tivissa?
A: Tivissa offers a diverse network of around 30 hiking routes. These range from easy walks suitable for families to more challenging treks through the karstic mountain ranges.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Tivissa's hiking trails?
A: The terrain in Tivissa is predominantly karstic, featuring rugged relief with limestone and dolomite rocks. You'll encounter a wild Mediterranean environment with varied landscapes including springs, wetlands, natural ponds, and plateaus, often providing panoramic views.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for families in Tivissa?
A: Yes, Tivissa has several easy routes perfect for families. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10419424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Sant Blai loop from Tivissa&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 3.5 miles long and takes less than two hours. This route leads to the historic 19th-century hermitage and was notably the first GR route established in Catalonia.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while hiking in Tivissa?
A: Hikers in Tivissa can explore various natural features. The region is known for its karstic caves, such as the Llena Caves and the Caves of Vilella. You might also discover springs, wetlands, and plateaus. For a specific highlight, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llop Marí Cove and Cave&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4391952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de la Sabinosa&lt;/a&gt; waterfall.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Tivissa?
A: Yes, many of Tivissa's trails are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llena Caves – View of Tivissa loop from Tivissa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.6-mile path offering views of the village and traversing the unique karstic landscape.

Q: What historical landmarks can be found along the trails?
A: Tivissa's trails often integrate rich historical elements. You can find ancient Iberian settlements, abandoned villages, old watchtowers, and the remains of Sant Blai de Tivissa castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3064827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Roca&lt;/a&gt; is another significant historical and religious site in the area.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Tivissa?
A: The hiking routes in Tivissa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 280 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse and rugged natural landscapes, the panoramic views that can stretch to the Costa Daurada, and the integration of historical sites along the trails.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The karstic mountain ranges of Tivissa and Vandellòs provide extraordinary panoramic views. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e953826938" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morral de Pena-Roja and Voltes de la Llena Trail&lt;/a&gt; are known for their stunning viewpoints overlooking the Pena-Roja valley and beyond.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Tivissa?
A: Tivissa offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. The wild Mediterranean climate makes spring and autumn particularly ideal, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be warm, but higher elevation trails or early morning starts are still enjoyable.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, Tivissa caters to experienced hikers with several difficult routes. While the majority are moderate, there are 5 difficult trails available for those seeking a greater challenge. These routes often involve significant elevation changes and rugged terrain, typical of the karstic mountains.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to caves in Tivissa?
A: Yes, the karstic landscape of Tivissa is dotted with caves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930475" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llena Caves – View of Tivissa loop from Tivissa&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate hike that takes you past some of these natural formations. The region's geological history means you might even find fossils in the limestone rocks.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The Muntanyes de Tivissa-Vandellòs Protected Natural Area is home to diverse wildlife. Hikers might spot various species of wheatears and whitethroats, and numerous birds of prey soaring above the crags. Mammals such as badgers, foxes, weasels, and genets also inhabit the area, though they are often more elusive.
tips: Different fruit trees are grown in Tivisa that become a true visual spectacle in spring. Almond trees, peach trees and cherry trees abound. At this point in particular you will find several fields of cherry trees that you cannot miss during the flowering season.

It is a balma 25 meters wide by 7.86 meters deep and 8 meters high. The floor of the balma is on an inclined plane of 14º and inside there are some drips that fall from the ceiling, in addition we also find some climbing routes. Going up from Tivissa, on the curve of the road before the balma, we find another balma, this one circular in shape of about 5 meters in diameter but only about 3 meters deep, in the set of both known as Llena caves.

Important crossroads where the Llena climb, the Camí de la Llena, the Camí de l'ermita de Sant Blai and the Benet shortcut come together to go up to la Tossa.
Magnificent panorama towards the SO.

The hermitage of San Blas is located in a kind of recreational area, in the middle of nature. It was built in the 19th century and is 20 meters long. Gothic in style, it has six chapels on each side. In this place, in addition, there is a spring of water.

The Portal de l'Era is one of the three old access points to the walled town of Tivissa, together with the Portal d'Avall and the Portal de la Raval. Few traces remain of the walls and the castle, built in the 14th century.

faqs.question: Is the Morral de Pena-Roja and Voltes de la Llena Trail a hiking route or a climbing area?
faqs.answer: While Morral de Pena-Roja is well-known as a rock climbing area with various technical routes, the "Morral de Pena-Roja and Voltes de la Llena Trail" described here is a dedicated hiking route. It allows you to explore the beautiful landscape, including areas near the climbing walls, without needing climbing gear.

faqs.question: How challenging is this trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. It covers a distance of approximately 15.1 km (9.4 miles) with an elevation gain of around 547 meters (1,795 feet), typically taking about 4 hours and 42 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a variety of sights, including the picturesque Cherry Orchards of Tivissa, the intriguing Llena Caves, and the historic Portal de l'Era. You'll also pass by the serene Hermitage of Sant Blai.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features the Coll de la Llena Viewpoint, offering scenic vistas of the surrounding landscape.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring (for the cherry blossoms) and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking. Summers can be hot, so an early start is recommended if hiking during warmer months. Winter conditions are usually mild, but always check the weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to ensure you clean up after them. Be mindful of the moderate difficulty and potential rocky sections for your pet.

faqs.question: Are there any fees or permits required for hiking in the Muntanyes de Tivissa-Vandellòs area?
faqs.answer: As of now, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or access the general Muntanyes de Tivissa-Vandellòs area. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route, meaning you'll end up back where you started. While the exact starting point can vary, a common approach is to begin near Tivissa. Parking is typically available in or near the village, often close to the approach for the Morral de Pena-Roja area.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, characteristic of the Mediterranean landscape. You can expect a mix of dirt paths, rocky sections, and possibly some gravel tracks. The area around Morral de Pena-Roja is known for its limestone rock, which you'll encounter on the hiking paths as well.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Morral de Pena-Roja. Tivissa, Voltes de la Llena, and Sender de Tivissa paths, among others, as you navigate the area.

faqs.question: What facilities are available near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The nearby village of Tivissa offers basic amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and potentially some accommodation options. It's a good place to grab a coffee before your hike or a meal afterward.
